text.title: Junior AI Co-Pilot Curriculum Procedure
text.summary: Six-step procedure from junior assessment to graduation.

step.name: assess-junior
step.action: Score baseline on the rubric items (probe 3 of 10). Identify modules to compress vs. extend.
step.decision-gate: If baseline ≥80% pre-program, run rubric only and skip the curriculum.

step.name: tailor-modules
step.action: Populate curriculum.json. Customise exercises to the team's real codebase (read-only at first, sandbox by week 3).
step.decision-gate: Curriculum must validate before sharing with the junior.

step.name: assign-exercises
step.action: Per week, assign one module + exercise + reflection deadline. Provide the mentor checklist for the upcoming 30-min review.
step.output: weekly tasks in tracker; reflection doc stub created.
step.decision-gate: If junior cannot start the exercise within 24h, the module spec is unclear — revise.

step.name: weekly-review
step.action: 30-min sync using the mentor checklist. Score the week's rubric items as pass/fail/needs-rework. Surface 1-2 talking points for next week.
step.output: review notes appended to curriculum.json runbook.
step.decision-gate: If 2 consecutive weeks have failed rubric items, pause progression and remediate.

step.name: score-rubric
step.action: Score all rubric items binary. Note any items requiring remediation.
step.output: final-rubric.json with per-item pass/fail + evidence link.
step.decision-gate: 100% pass required for graduation. Otherwise → step 6 with remediation week scheduled.

step.name: graduate-or-remediate
step.action: Graduate (announce in team channel; unblock unsupervised co-pilot use) or schedule a one-week remediation focused on failed items.
step.decision-gate: After 2 remediations, escalate to engineering manager — may not be a fit for co-pilot-augmented role.
